title: Claude Research Mission
description: A spy mission to do deep research with Claude!

scenes.speech: Research Agent! Today's mission is real research with Claude! 🕵️📚
scenes.title: Mission Briefing: Research Operation
scenes.content: Your task is to investigate one topic from multiple angles using Claude and summarize it in three lines. Three success conditions: confirm at least one source, look at both pros and cons, and write the conclusion in your own words.

scenes.speech: Claude is especially good at organizing long text!
scenes.title: What is Claude?
scenes.content: Claude is an AI that excels at reading long documents and comparing several pieces of information. It really shines when you paste material and discuss it, not just send a short question.
scenes.highlight:
Strong points:
- Summarizing long text
- Comparing viewpoints
- Sorting arguments

scenes.speech: Turn the topic into a 'question' to start the research!
scenes.title: Turn It into a Question
scenes.content: A topic like 'AI' is too broad. Reframe it as 'Will AI take over kids' homework?' so you can research both sides of a clear question.
scenes.highlight:
Question shapes:
- Is X really true?
- What did X change?
- What does X need?

scenes.speech: Don't ask one side! Hear both for and against!
scenes.title: Both Sides at Once
scenes.content: Ask Claude for 'three reasons in favor' and 'three reasons against' separately, then ask for 'a conclusion balancing both'. It clears your thinking.
scenes.highlight:
How to ask:
1. 3 pro reasons
2. 3 con reasons
3. Your own conclusion

scenes.speech: Paste the source and read it together!
scenes.title: Source Reading
scenes.content: Paste a news article or book excerpt and ask 'Give me three key points from this'. Long text becomes easy. Keep the source link or page for later checking.
scenes.highlight:
How to ask:
- Three key points
- Explain hard words
- Impact on me

scenes.speech: Doubt the AI too! Always verify with your own head!
scenes.title: Fact Check
scenes.content: AI can be wrong, so verify important numbers or names with another source. Asking Claude back, 'What's the basis for this?' is also a useful check.
scenes.highlight:
Check methods:
- Numbers in the original data
- Names on official sites
- Dates in newspaper records

scenes.speech: Finally, summarize in three lines using your own words!
scenes.title: Three-Line Summary
scenes.content: Line 1: the question. Line 2: what you found. Line 3: your opinion. This template makes even short reports easy to read.

scenes.speech: Mission complete! Submit your 3-line summary to earn 460 points! 🎯
scenes.title: Mission Complete!
scenes.content: Research isn't about collecting answers — it's about training the muscle of asking. Combine AI with fact-checking and you'll know things faster and deeper than anyone.
